maml:description.maml:para: Clears the proxy configuration for the Defender for Identity sensor.
This function clears the proxy configuration for the Defender for Identity sensor.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet isn't run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet isn't run.

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example clears the proxy configuration for the Defender for Identity sensor.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the configuration for various Defender for Identity post-deployment required settings.
The `Get-MDIConfiguration` function gets the configuration for various Defender for Identity post-deployment required settings.
Specifies the mode to use. You must specify one of the following values:
- `Domain`: Collect settings from the Group Policy objects
- `LocalMachine`: Collect settings from the local machine
Specifies the configuration to get. You can specify one or more of the following values:
- `All` (all configurations)
- `AdfsAuditing`
- `AdRecycleBin`
- `AdvancedAuditPolicyCAs`
- `AdvancedAuditPolicyDCs`
- `CAAuditing`
- `ConfigurationContainerAuditing`
- `DeletedObjectsContainerPermission`
- `DomainObjectAuditing`
- `EntraConnectAuditing`
- `RemoteSAM`
- `DomainObjectAuditing`
- `NTLMAuditing`
- `ProcessorPerformance`
Specifies the name of the domain to run the command against. This parameter is optional and defaults to the user's DNS domain.
Specifies a prefix for the Group Policy Objects (GPO) names to be searched. Use this parameter for GPO naming convention.
Specifies the name of the service account to use for the EntraConnectAuditing or RemoteSAM configuration. This parameter is mandatory.
Specifies the name of the server to run the command against. This parameter is optional and defaults to the PDC Emulator in the domain.
Specifies the configuration to get. You can specify one or more of the following values:
- `All` (all configurations)
- `AdfsAuditing`
- `AdRecycleBin`
- `AdvancedAuditPolicyCAs`
- `AdvancedAuditPolicyDCs`
- `CAAuditing`
- `ConfigurationContainerAuditing`
- `DeletedObjectsContainerPermission`
- `DomainObjectAuditing`
- `EntraConnectAuditing`
- `RemoteSAM`
- `DomainObjectAuditing`
- `NTLMAuditing`
- `ProcessorPerformance`
Specifies the name of the domain to run the command against. This parameter is optional and defaults to the user's DNS domain.
Specifies a prefix for the Group Policy Objects (GPO) names to be searched. Use this parameter for GPO naming convention.
Specifies the name of the service account to use for the EntraConnectAuditing or RemoteSAM configuration. This parameter is mandatory.
Specifies the mode to use. You must specify one of the following values:
- `Domain`: Collect settings from the Group Policy objects
- `LocalMachine`: Collect settings from the local machine
Specifies the name of the server to run the command against. This parameter is optional and defaults to the PDC Emulator in the domain.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code:
Get-MDIConfiguration -Mode LocalMachine -Configuration NTLMAuditing
Name Status Details ---- ------ ------- NTLMAuditing True {@{Path=HKLM:\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netlogon\Parameters\; Name=AuditNTLMInDomain...

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example returns the `NTLMAuditing` configuration for the local machine.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code:
Get-MDIConfiguration -Mode Domain -Configuration All -GpoNamePrefix 'CONTOSO' -Identity 'mdisvc01'
Configuration Mode Status Details ------------- ---- ------ ------- AdfsAuditing Domain True {@{Account=Everyone; SecurityIdentifier=S-1-1-0; AccessMask=48; Access... AdRecycleBin Domain True Active Directory Recycle Bin is Enabled AdvancedAuditPolicyCAs Domain False 'CONTOSO - Advanced Audit Policy for CAs' - GPO not found AdvancedAuditPolicyDCs Domain False 'CONTOSO - Advanced Audit Policy for DCs' - GPO not found CAAuditing Domain False 'CONTOSO - Auditing for CAs' - GPO not found ConfigurationContainerAuditing Domain True {@{Account=Everyone; SecurityIdentifier=S-1-1-0; AccessMask=32; Access... DomainObjectAuditing Domain True {@{Account=Everyone; SecurityIdentifier=S-1-1-0; AccessMask=852331; Ac... EntraConnectAuditing Domain False 'CONTOSO - Advanced Audit and URA Policy for Entra Connect' - GPO not ... NTLMAuditing Domain False 'CONTOSO - NTLM Auditing for DCs' - GPO not found ProcessorPerformance Domain False 'CONTOSO - Processor Performance' - GPO not found RemoteSAM Domain False 'CONTOSO - Remote SAM Access' - GPO not found

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example returns all configurations for the domain (including GPOs and their links), using the `CONTOSO` prefix to search for the GPO names.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves the proxy configuration for the Defender for Identity sensor.
This function retrieves the proxy configuration for the Defender for Identity sensor. If the sensor configuration can't be read, the function returns `$null`.
Returns a `PSCustomObject` representing the proxy configuration for the Defender for Identity sensor. If the sensor configuration can't be read, the function returns `$null`.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code:
Get-MDISensorProxyConfiguration
IsProxyEnabled : True IsAuthenticationProxyEnabled : True Url : https://myProxy.contoso.com UserName : CONTOSO\proxyUser EncryptedUserPasswordData : PgYTSj0SW6fNx...== CertificateThumbprint : A7A60C2841A0104C9E199C786849228C56223E03

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example returns the applied proxy configuration for the Defender for Identity sensor.

maml:description.maml:para: Generates a report of MDI domain configuration and saves it as an HTML and a detailed JSON file.

Generates a report of MDI domain configuration and saves it as HTML file and detailed JSON files. The report validates the domain SACLs and the presence of the MDI related configuration GPOs. The HTML report includes a table with the status for each configuration and a link to the detailed JSON data.
Specifies the directory where to save the HTML and JSON report files.
Specifies the name of the domain to run the command against. This parameter is optional and defaults to the user's DNS domain.
Specifies a prefix for the Group Policy Objects (GPO) names to be searched. Use this parameter for GPO naming convention.
Specifies the name of the service account used for the EntraConnectAuditing or RemoteSAM configuration. This parameter is mandatory in the `Domain` mode.
Specifies the mode to use. You must specify one of the following values:
- `Domain`: Collect settings from the Group Policy objects
- `LocalMachine`: Collect settings from the local machine
If specified, opens the HTML report after it's generated.
Specifies the name of the domain to run the command against. This parameter is optional and defaults to the user's DNS domain.
Specifies a prefix for the Group Policy Objects (GPO) names to be searched. Use this parameter for GPO naming convention.
Specifies the name of the service account used for the EntraConnectAuditing or RemoteSAM configuration. This parameter is mandatory in the `Domain` mode.
Specifies the mode to use. You must specify one of the following values:
- `Domain`: Collect settings from the Group Policy objects
- `LocalMachine`: Collect settings from the local machine
If specified, opens the HTML report after it's generated.
Specifies the directory where to save the HTML and JSON report files.

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example generates a report of MDI domain configuration, saves it as HTML and JSON files in the `C:\Reports` directory, and opens the HTML report after in your default browser.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new Directory Service Account (DSA).

This function creates a new Directory Service Account (DSA). It can create either a GMSA (Group Managed Service Account) or a standard service account based on the provided parameters, and sets the required permissions for the Deleted Objects container.
Specifies the name of the service account to be created. This parameter is mandatory and must be a string with a length between 1 and 16 characters.

Specifies the base distinguished name (DN) for the service account. This parameter is optional and for standard accounts it defaults to the Users container in your domain (for example, "CN=Users,DC=Contoso,DC=com"). GMSAs are created under the Managed Service Accounts container.
Specifies the name of the domain to run the command against. This parameter is optional and defaults to the user's DNS domain.
If specified, forces the creation of a standard service account instead of a GMSA.
Specifies the name of the GMSA group. This parameter is mandatory and must be a string with a length between 1 and 28 characters.
Specifies the name of the server to run the command against. This parameter is optional and defaults to the PDC Emulator in the domain.

Specifies the base distinguished name (DN) for the service account. This parameter is optional and for standard accounts it defaults to the Users container in your domain (for example, "CN=Users,DC=Contoso,DC=com"). GMSAs are created under the Managed Service Accounts container.
Specifies the name of the domain to run the command against. This parameter is optional and defaults to the user's DNS domain.
If specified, forces the creation of a standard service account instead of a GMSA.
Specifies the name of the GMSA group. This parameter is mandatory and must be a string with a length between 1 and 28 characters.
Specifies the name of the service account to be created. This parameter is mandatory and must be a string with a length between 1 and 16 characters.
Specifies the name of the server to run the command against. This parameter is optional and defaults to the PDC Emulator in the domain.
By default, the cmdlet returns `$true` when the specified Directory Service Account (DSA) has been created successfully.

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example creates a new GMSA service account with the name "mdiSvc01" and the GMSA group name "mdiSvcGroup01".
This example creates a new standard service account with the name "mdiSvc02".

maml:description.maml:para: Sets the configuration for various Defender for Identity post-deployment required settings.
The `Set-MDIConfiguration` function sets the configuration for various Defender for Identity post-deployment required settings.
Specifies the mode to use. You must specify one of the following values:
- `Domain`: Collect settings from the Group Policy objects
- `LocalMachine`: Collect settings from the local machine
Specifies the configuration to set. You can specify one or more of the following values:
- `All` (all configurations)
- `AdfsAuditing`
- `AdRecycleBin`
- `AdvancedAuditPolicyCAs`
- `AdvancedAuditPolicyDCs`
- `CAAuditing`
- `ConfigurationContainerAuditing`
- `DeletedObjectsContainerPermission`
- `DomainObjectAuditing`
- `EntraConnectAuditing`
- `RemoteSAM`
- `DomainObjectAuditing`
- `NTLMAuditing`
- `ProcessorPerformance`
If specified, the GPOs are created and kept as disabled. This parameter is valid only in `Domain` mode.
Specifies the name of the domain to run the command against. This parameter is optional and defaults to the user's DNS domain.
If specified, set the configuration or create GPOs without validating the current state.

Specifies a prefix for the Group Policy Objects (GPO) names to be created. Use this parameter for GPO naming convention. Note: Because the GPO's display name may not be unique, an error is returned if another GPO in the domain has the same display name.
Specifies the name of the service account to use for the EntraConnectAuditing or RemoteSAM configuration. This parameter is mandatory.
Specifies the name of the server to run the command against. This parameter is optional and defaults to the PDC Emulator in the domain.
If specified, don't create GPO links. This parameter is valid only in `Domain` mode.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet isn't run.
Specifies the configuration to set. You can specify one or more of the following values:
- `All` (all configurations)
- `AdfsAuditing`
- `AdRecycleBin`
- `AdvancedAuditPolicyCAs`
- `AdvancedAuditPolicyDCs`
- `CAAuditing`
- `ConfigurationContainerAuditing`
- `DeletedObjectsContainerPermission`
- `DomainObjectAuditing`
- `EntraConnectAuditing`
- `RemoteSAM`
- `DomainObjectAuditing`
- `NTLMAuditing`
- `ProcessorPerformance`
If specified, the GPOs are created and kept as disabled. This parameter is valid only in `Domain` mode.
Specifies the name of the domain to run the command against. This parameter is optional and defaults to the user's DNS domain.
If specified, set the configuration or create GPOs without validating the current state.

Specifies a prefix for the Group Policy Objects (GPO) names to be created. Use this parameter for GPO naming convention. Note: Because the GPO's display name may not be unique, an error is returned if another GPO in the domain has the same display name.
Specifies the name of the service account to use for the EntraConnectAuditing or RemoteSAM configuration. This parameter is mandatory.
Specifies the mode to use. You must specify one of the following values:
- `Domain`: Collect settings from the Group Policy objects
- `LocalMachine`: Collect settings from the local machine
Specifies the name of the server to run the command against. This parameter is optional and defaults to the PDC Emulator in the domain.
If specified, don't create GPO links. This parameter is valid only in `Domain` mode.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet isn't run.

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example sets the `NTLMAuditing` configuration for the local machine.
This example sets all configurations for the domain, creating the GPOs and linking them.
This example sets all configurations for the domain, creating the GPOs with a `CONTOSO` prefix in their name, without linking the GPOs

maml:description.maml:para: Sets the proxy configuration for the Defender for Identity sensor.

This cmdlet sets the proxy configuration for the Defender for Identity sensor. If the ProxyUrl parameter is null or empty, the function clears the proxy configuration. If you specify the ProxyCredential parameter, the function encrypts the password and stores it in the sensor configuration file.
The URL of the proxy server.
The credentials to use to authenticate with the proxy server. You can use `Get-Credential` to create a credential object.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet isn't run.
The credentials to use to authenticate with the proxy server. You can use `Get-Credential` to create a credential object.
The URL of the proxy server.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet isn't run.

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example sets the proxy configuration for the Defender for Identity sensor to use the specified proxy server and credentials.
This example sets the proxy configuration for the Defender for Identity sensor to use the specified proxy server without any credentials.

maml:description.maml:para: Validates the configuration for various Defender for Identity post-deployment required settings.
This cmdlet validates the Defender for Identity configuration for settings specified by the Configuration parameter.
Specifies the mode to use. You must specify one of the following values:
- `Domain`: Collect settings from the Group Policy objects
- `LocalMachine`: Collect settings from the local machine
Specifies the configuration to test. You can specify one or more of the following values:
- `All` (all configurations)
- `AdfsAuditing`
- `AdRecycleBin`
- `AdvancedAuditPolicyCAs`
- `AdvancedAuditPolicyDCs`
- `CAAuditing`
- `ConfigurationContainerAuditing`
- `DeletedObjectsContainerPermission`
- `DomainObjectAuditing`
- `EntraConnectAuditing`
- `RemoteSAM`
- `DomainObjectAuditing`
- `NTLMAuditing`
- `ProcessorPerformance`
Specifies the name of the domain to run the command against. This parameter is optional and defaults to the user's DNS domain.
Specifies a prefix for the Group Policy Objects (GPO) names to be searched and tested. Use this parameter for GPO naming convention.
Specifies the name of the service account to use for the EntraConnectAuditing or RemoteSAM configuration. This parameter is mandatory.
Specifies the name of the server to run the command against. This parameter is optional and defaults to the PDC Emulator in the domain.
Specifies the configuration to test. You can specify one or more of the following values:
- `All` (all configurations)
- `AdfsAuditing`
- `AdRecycleBin`
- `AdvancedAuditPolicyCAs`
- `AdvancedAuditPolicyDCs`
- `CAAuditing`
- `ConfigurationContainerAuditing`
- `DeletedObjectsContainerPermission`
- `DomainObjectAuditing`
- `EntraConnectAuditing`
- `RemoteSAM`
- `DomainObjectAuditing`
- `NTLMAuditing`
- `ProcessorPerformance`
Specifies the name of the domain to run the command against. This parameter is optional and defaults to the user's DNS domain.
Specifies a prefix for the Group Policy Objects (GPO) names to be searched and tested. Use this parameter for GPO naming convention.
Specifies the name of the service account to use for the EntraConnectAuditing or RemoteSAM configuration. This parameter is mandatory.
Specifies the name of the server to run the command against. This parameter is optional and defaults to the PDC Emulator in the domain.
Specifies the mode to use. You must specify one of the following values:
- `Domain`: Collect settings from the Group Policy objects
- `LocalMachine`: Collect settings from the local machine
The cmdlet returns `$true` when the settings are configured as required. Otherwise, it returns `$false`.

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example validates the `NTLMAuditing` configuration for the local machine.
This example validates the `NTLMAuditing` configurations for the domain, in a GPO with the prefix of `CONTOSO` in its name.
This example validates all configurations for the domain, including SACLs and GPOs.

maml:description.maml:para: Validates the permissions and delegation of a Directory Service Account (DSA).
This function validates the permissions and delegation of a Directory Service Account (DSA) by checking its ACLs, manager assignments, and sensitive group membership.
Specifies the identity of the Directory Service Account (DSA) to test.
If specified, returns detailed information about the validation status.
Specifies the name of the domain to run the command against. This parameter is optional and defaults to the user's DNS domain.
Specifies the name of the server to run the command against. This parameter is optional and defaults to the PDC Emulator in the domain.
If specified, returns detailed information about the validation status.
Specifies the name of the domain to run the command against. This parameter is optional and defaults to the user's DNS domain.
Specifies the identity of the Directory Service Account (DSA) to test.
Specifies the name of the server to run the command against. This parameter is optional and defaults to the PDC Emulator in the domain.
By default, the cmdlet returns `$true` when the specified Directory Service Account (DSA) has the correct permissions and delegation.
When you use the Detailed parameter, the cmdlet returns a custom object containing the status and details of each test.

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example returns a boolean value indicating whether the specified Directory Service Account (DSA) has any issues with its permissions and delegation.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example returns a detailed output for the Directory Service Account (DSA) permissions and delegation validations.

maml:description.maml:para: Validates the connection to the Defender for Identity Sensor API.
This function validates the connection to the Defender for Identity Sensor API using the current configuration or bypassing it with custom parameters.
Switch parameter to bypass the current configuration and use custom parameters.
Specifies the credentials to use for the proxy server.
Specifies the URL of the proxy server to use for the connection.
Specifies the URL of the Defender for Identity Sensor API to test.
Specifies the sensor version installed, or planned to install, on the device. Accepts one of the following values:
- `Classic`
- `Unified`
Switch parameter to bypass the current configuration and use custom parameters.
Specifies the credentials to use for the proxy server.
Specifies the URL of the proxy server to use for the connection.
Specifies the URL of the Defender for Identity Sensor API to test.
Specifies the sensor version installed, or planned to install, on the device. Accepts one of the following values:
- `Classic`
- `Unified`
The cmdlet returns `$true` when the connection to the Defender for Identity Sensor API is successful or `$false` when it fails.

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example tests the connection to the Defender for Identity Sensor API using the current configuration of the installed sensor.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: $connectionParams = @{ BypassConfiguration = $true SensorApiUrl = 'https://contososensorapi.atp.azure.com' SensorType = 'Classic' ProxyUrl = 'https://myproxy.contoso.com:8080' ProxyCredential = $credential } Test-MDISensorApiConnection @connectionParams

dev:remarks.maml:para: This example tests the connection to the Defender for Identity Sensor API using custom parameters.
